name: Medicine Buddha Practice (Bhaiṣajyaguru Sādhana)
primary_purpose: Healing of physical and mental illness; alleviation of suffering for self and all beings
visualizations: Medicine Buddha appears in the space before you, seated on a lotus and moon disc
His body is transparent lapis-lazuli blue (#1B4F8B), radiating healing light in all directions
His right hand rests in the mudrā of supreme giving over his right knee, holding a myrobalan (arura) plant — the king of medicinal herbs
His left hand rests in his lap in meditation mudrā, holding a lapis bowl filled with healing nectar
Lapis-blue healing light streams from his heart and bowl into your crown, filling your body and dissolving all illness, blockages, and impurities into black smoke that leaves your body
Light radiates outward to all sentient beings — humans, animals, and beings in all realms — relieving their suffering completely
benefits: Healing of physical illnesses of body
Healing of mental illnesses — anxiety, depression, obsession
Relief from chronic pain and suffering
Purification of negative karma causing illness
Blessing of medicinal herbs, water, and substances
direction: Eastern Pure Land of Lapis Vaiḍūrya (Pure Lapis Lazuli)
twelve_vows_summary: May my body shine with lapis light
May beings be free from illness
May beings have their needs fulfilled
May beings who have gone astray find the right path
May beings born with defects be healed
May beings be free from disease and poverty
May women who wish to renounce become men (per traditional vow)
May beings be free from Mara's snares
May those in prison or disaster be freed
May the hungry and thirsty be fed
May beings in need of clothing receive it
best_times: On Medicine Buddha's feast days (8th day of the lunar month in some traditions)
dedication: By this merit, may all beings be free from illness, suffering, and the causes of suffering. May they attain the state of Medicine Buddha, the King of Lapis Light.
